description: Reusable Dota custom-game design/engineering patterns, learned from shipping games. Tower-defense/maze + backend patterns from Spin TD Reborn & GemTD; Panorama UI/animation, progression, economy and backend patterns distilled from a deep analysis of 18 top games (Dota IMBA, Guarding Athena, Angel Arena Reborn, Pudge Wars, Life In Arena, Horde Mode, Overthrow, and more — see docs panorama/animations-cookbook, panorama/hud-ux-patterns, scripting/custom-game-architecture).

patterns.name: Creep pathing via trigger waypoints
patterns.summary: Move creeps along a road using numbered map trigger volumes + a waypoint table (Spin TD Reborn).
patterns.technique: Place named brush move-trigger volumes along the road; each OnStartTouch calls a map vscript that advances the unit to the next point in a global WAYPOINTS table via ExecuteOrderFromTable({UnitIndex, OrderType=DOTA_UNIT_ORDER_MOVE_TO_POSITION, Position=WAYPOINTS[next]}). An end trigger = leak.

patterns.name: Creep pathing via computed path + per-unit think
patterns.summary: Compute the route once, then each creep follows it with MoveToPosition and a think (GemTD).
patterns.technique: Build a path table (e.g. from an A* solve) and store it; on each creep run a think that calls unit:MoveToPosition(path[target]) and increments target when within ~range. No brush triggers needed — fully programmatic (this is what scaffold_td emits).

patterns.name: Maze validation with grid A*
patterns.summary: Let players build a maze but never fully block the creep path (the core maze-TD rule).
patterns.technique: Keep a boolean walkable grid. To validate a build, temporarily mark the target cell(s) blocked and run A* from the spawn cell to the finish cell; allow the build only if a path still exists, otherwise refuse. Recompute the creep path from the same grid after each successful build. scaffold_td --maze emits this.

patterns.name: Declarative wave table
patterns.summary: Define waves as data, not code.
patterns.technique: A table of Wave(creep, total, perSpawn, gold, ...) / Boss(...) entries (boss every ~5). A single spawner loop iterates it: spawn perSpawn each interval until total, then schedule the next wave.

patterns.name: vscript HTTP backend
patterns.summary: Custom games can call an external HTTP server from server-side vscript (leaderboards, save validation, anti-cheat).
patterns.technique: const req = CreateHTTPRequestScriptVM("GET"|"POST", url); req:SetHTTPRequestAbsoluteTimeoutMS(20000); req:Send(result => { local body = result["Body"]; ... }). Push results to the client via CustomNetTables:SetTableValue. GemTD uses it for save codes + an anti-cheat verdict (server returns 'blocked' -> SetGameWinner).

patterns.name: Save / load codes
patterns.summary: Persist player progress as an encoded string (or via the HTTP backend).
patterns.technique: Serialize state to JSON (dkjson), compress (libdeflate), sign/encode (sha2 / aes / base64) into a shareable code; reverse to restore. GemTD pairs this with the HTTP backend.
patterns.source: GemTD libs (libdeflate, sha2, aeslua, base64, dkjson)

patterns.name: Map anchors by name
patterns.summary: Bind vscript logic to specific map positions by naming point entities.
patterns.technique: Name point entities (info_target / path_track) in the map (e.g. path0..pathN, spawn, finish); read them from vscript with Entities:FindByName(nil, "path7"):GetAbsOrigin(). map_build's `paths` names nodes name_0.. which you can resolve this way.

patterns.name: Per-unit AI scripts
patterns.summary: One AI script file per tower/boss keeps complex behaviour modular.
patterns.technique: Give a unit a vscript (KV 'vscripts' / AddNewModifier think) per archetype: scripts/vscripts/unit_scripts/ai_tower_X.lua, ai_boss_Y.lua. Each owns that unit's targeting/abilities.

patterns.name: Custom projectiles
patterns.summary: A projectile/physics library for tower shots beyond ProjectileManager.
patterns.technique: Both games ship a projectiles/physics lib (homing, AoE, custom motion) layered over the engine. For simple cases prefer the built-in ProjectileManager.CreateLinearProjectile / CreateTrackingProjectile.
patterns.api: ProjectileManager (built-in) or a custom physics lib

patterns.name: State-class CSS animation (JS owns state, CSS owns motion)
patterns.summary: The #1 Panorama UI idiom across shipping games: JS only flips a class; the keyframe/transition defined in CSS does the animation.
patterns.technique: Define the animated state in CSS (e.g. `#Win.setvisible{ transform: translate3d(0,0,0); }` with a transition on the base rule). From JS just `panel.SetHasClass('setvisible', show)` / `panel.AddClass(...)`. Use `visibility: collapse` in the hidden state to also drop it from layout + hit-testing. Keeps JS tiny and motion declarative/reversible.
patterns.api: Panel.SetHasClass / AddClass / RemoveClass, CSS transition/@keyframes, visibility: collapse
patterns.source: Dota IMBA, Guarding Athena, Pudge Wars (battlepass.css, notification, store)

patterns.name: Pop-in toast with scale overshoot
patterns.summary: Punchy kill-feed / loot / notification entrance that overshoots then settles.
patterns.technique: @keyframes scaling pre-transform-scale2d 0.2 -> 1.5 -> 1.0 over ~0.4s (or scaleX/Y 2 -> 1 for a 'slam'). pre-transform-scale2d animates without affecting layout. Stack toasts upward by flipping the container with `transform: scaleY(-1)` (and each child back).
patterns.source: Guarding Athena notification/styles.css; Pudge Wars barebones_notifications.css

patterns.name: Rarity 'breathe' glow via animated inset box-shadow
patterns.summary: Cheap, GPU-friendly pulsing border to mark item/reward rarity tiers.
patterns.technique: One infinite keyframe per tier animating an inset box-shadow radius, e.g. `@keyframes breathe_immortal {0%{box-shadow: inset #e6b800 0 0 1px 0;}50%{box-shadow: inset #e6b800 0 0 4px 0;}100%{...1px...}}`; JS just adds `.immortal_border`. Same idiom drives 'can level up' attractor pulses.

patterns.name: Shine-sweep glint over an icon
patterns.summary: A diagonal light stripe that sweeps across an icon for 'new'/'ready' feedback.
patterns.technique: Overlay a gradient-stripe panel (`background-color: gradient(linear, 100% 0%, 0% 100%, from(#00000000), color-stop(0.45,#fff), color-stop(0.55,#fff), to(#00000000))`) and animate it with a keyframe translating translateX/translateY across the icon when a `.do_shine` class is added.

patterns.name: 3D fly-in modal (perspective + translate + scale)
patterns.summary: Clean modal-open effect that zooms/rotates a panel into place from off-axis.
patterns.technique: On the base rule set `perspective: 1000; perspective-origin: ...; transform: translateX(-120px) translateY(-60px); pre-transform-scale2d: 0.95; opacity: 0;` with a transition; the `.Show` state resets transform/scale/opacity to neutral. For drawers, use `transform: translate3d(100%,0,0)` <-> `translate3d(0,0,0)`.
patterns.api: perspective, perspective-origin, transform translate3d, pre-transform-scale2d, transition
patterns.source: Guarding Athena store/styles.css; Dota IMBA/Pudge Wars BattlepassWindow

patterns.name: Parameterized keyframes via @define constants
patterns.summary: Declare timing/iteration once and reference the symbol everywhere so a whole set of animations shares one tunable clock.
patterns.technique: `@define TotalLength: 3s; @define TimingFunc: ease;` then `animation-duration: TotalLength; animation-timing-function: TimingFunc;`. Also used for color theming (`@define slot0color: #0038AC;`).
patterns.source: Pudge Wars pudgewars_tutorial.css; Guarding Athena dashboard css

patterns.name: Radial cooldown sweep via style.clip radial()
patterns.summary: Render a custom circular cooldown/charge sweep without particles.
patterns.technique: Set `panel.style.clip = 'radial(50% 50%, 0deg, ' + degrees + 'deg)'` from JS, polling the ability/buff remaining time at ~10Hz. Overlays cleanly on a circular icon.

patterns.name: Prime-and-subscribe net-table key helper
patterns.summary: Keep a panel in sync with ONE net-table row: invoke the callback with the current value, then subscribe for changes to that key.
patterns.technique: function SubscribeToNetTableKey(t,key,cb){ var v=CustomNetTables.GetTableValue(t,key); if(v!=null) cb(v); CustomNetTables.SubscribeNetTableListener(t,function(_t,k,val){ if(k===key&&val!=null) cb(val); }); } — avoids the classic 'UI misses the first value' bug.
patterns.source: Dota IMBA; Guarding Athena (reactive effect variant)

patterns.name: RPC over custom game events (correlation id)
patterns.summary: Turn Dota's one-way custom events into request/response calls with per-call callbacks.
patterns.technique: Stamp an incrementing id on each SendCustomGameEventToServer payload, Subscribe, and Unsubscribe the moment a reply with the matching id arrives. Server replies via Send_ServerToPlayer with the same id. On the server, a coroutine.wrap+xpcall router can yield on CreateHTTPRequest before replying (crash-isolated RPC + backend bridge).
patterns.source: Dota IMBA createEventRequestCreator; Guarding Athena libraries/request.lua

patterns.name: Augment Valve's HUD instead of rebuilding it
patterns.summary: Reach into the built-in Dota HUD and inject custom panels to inherit Valve's styling/tooltips/art for free.
patterns.technique: var Shop = GameUI.Utils.FindDotaHudElement('shop'); var ItemBuild = Shop.FindChildTraverse('ItemBuild'); then $.CreatePanel('DOTAShopItem'/'DOTAAbilityImage', ...) into it and wire DOTAShowAbilityTooltip. Far less work/risk than reimplementing the shop or ability bar.
patterns.source: Dota IMBA (custom item builds injected into the native shop)

patterns.name: Bake server KeyValues to client JS modules
patterns.summary: Ship heroes/items/abilities/upgrades/shops KV to the UI as generated JS lookup tables so panels read tooltips/costs/icons locally with no round-trips.
patterns.technique: Pre-generate panorama/scripts/custom_game/kv/*.js (itemskv.js, abilityupgradeskv.js, shopskv.js, taskskv.js) as large object literals; include them and read directly. Regenerate as part of the build.

patterns.name: Deferred layout measurement for popups
patterns.summary: Position a popup/dropdown relative to its trigger AFTER layout is computed.
patterns.technique: Make the popup visible, then `$.Schedule(0.06, () => { let w = Math.max(menu.actuallayoutwidth, btn.actuallayoutwidth)/menu.actualuiscale_x; let pos = btn.GetPositionWithinWindow(); menu.style.x = ...; })`. The one-frame defer is required because measurements are 0 until laid out.

patterns.name: Reactive Panorama via a React/Solid reconciler
patterns.summary: Build complex HUDs as declarative components that auto-rerender on net-table changes, instead of imperative $.CreatePanel spaghetti.
patterns.technique: Bundle react-panorama (a React reconciler whose hostConfig maps JSX props to Panorama panels via $.CreatePanel/SetParent/MoveChildBefore) or SolidJS; put CustomNetTables listeners inside effects with onCleanup teardown. Heaviest setup, biggest payoff for large UIs.

patterns.name: Net-table-delivered server key as HTTP auth token
patterns.summary: Let the (sandboxed, secret-less) Panorama client authenticate to a real REST backend.
patterns.technique: The Lua server generates a per-server secret and pushes it via CustomNetTables('game_options','server_key'); the client reads it and sends it as an `X-Dota-Server-Key` header on $.AsyncWebRequest calls to your API (leaderboards, battlepass, armory, save persistence). Decouples persistence from the limited Lua HTTP layer.

patterns.name: Frame-count-keyed debounce for net-table writes
patterns.summary: Collapse many same-frame mutations of shared state into a single net-table push.
patterns.technique: In every setter call UpdateNetTables(); it stamps a per-entity flag like 'Update'..id..'_'..GetFrameCount() and, if unset, schedules a 0-delay Timer that clears it and pushes once. Multiple writes in one frame (gold+crystal+score from one kill) become one CustomNetTables:SetTableValue — no explicit dirty-flag plumbing.

patterns.name: Column-oriented 'zip' compression for big net tables
patterns.summary: Fit a large, growing list (e.g. a roguelike upgrade set) under the net-table size budget.
patterns.technique: Instead of N objects with repeated keys, emit one header row of field names + value-only rows (a matrix), json-encode, and replace the literal 'null' with '*' (the largest repeated token). Clients zip header to values to reconstruct.

patterns.name: Roguelike draw-3-pick-1 upgrade picker
patterns.summary: Offer a small random choice of upgrades at milestones (the auto-battler/roguelike reward loop).
patterns.technique: On a level/round milestone the server draws a rarity from a weighted pool, builds K candidate cards filtered by prerequisites/max-count/unit, publishes them to a CustomNetTables selection keyed by entindex; the UI shows them and sends the chosen id back. Stash unselected draws for later.

patterns.name: Majority-vote selection (difficulty / next mode)
patterns.summary: Let players vote during a lobby/end screen and resolve at a game-state change.
patterns.technique: Each client writes its choice into a net table (with a countdown); at PRE_GAME (or end) the server tallies votes and picks the majority (define a deterministic tie-break, e.g. lower difficulty). Auto-pick for players who didn't choose.
patterns.source: Guarding Athena (difficulty); Dota IMBA (end-game mode vote)

patterns.name: Class-based singleton modules with hot-reload guard
patterns.summary: Structure server code as modules that survive script_reload cleanly.
patterns.technique: Each system is `X = X or class({})` with an `init(bReload)` that only seeds state when not a reload, then (re)binds events through wrappers (GameEvent/CustomUIEvent/Timer). A central init.lua clears package.loaded on reload so modules re-run. Keeps live-reload safe for complex addons.

patterns.name: Custom combat-event toast feed
patterns.summary: Replace Valve's kill/gold/roshan toasts with a styled, animated feed.
patterns.technique: Server sends 'combat'/'upper'/'chat_line' events with a token-substitution message format ({s:player_name}, {d:int_*}, item-icon detection); the client renders angled (opacity-mask PNG), team-colored, gradient panels that fly in and collapse out via class toggles, stacked upward.
patterns.source: Dota IMBA combat_events.css; Guarding Athena notification.lua

patterns.name: Particle lifecycle: create → set CPs → ReleaseParticleIndex
patterns.summary: The pervasive one-shot FX idiom that avoids leaks without Lua bookkeeping.
patterns.technique: For fire-and-forget effects: local id = ParticleManager:CreateParticle(path, attach, parent); set control points; then ParticleManager:ReleaseParticleIndex(id) IMMEDIATELY. The particle plays to completion and the engine reclaims the index automatically. For persistent/looping FX you must keep the id and ParticleManager:DestroyParticle(id, false) yourself (or use modifier AddParticle).

patterns.name: Floating damage numbers via a message particle
patterns.summary: Damage/combat-text is a particle (msg_*.vpcf), not a UI element — encode the number into control points.
patterns.technique: CreateParticle('particles/msg_fx/msg_crit.vpcf' or msg_damage/msg_gold, PATTACH_CUSTOMORIGIN); SetParticleControlEnt(id, 0, target, PATTACH_OVERHEAD_FOLLOW, ...); CP1 = (0, value, digitStyle); CP2 = (duration, #digits+1, 0); CP3 = color; then ReleaseParticleIndex. Reuses Valve's overhead message FX.

patterns.name: Modifier-owned FX with AddParticle (auto-cleanup)
patterns.summary: Looping/aura FX that the engine destroys when the modifier ends — created client-side to skip replication.
patterns.technique: Inside a modifier, guard with if IsClient() then create the particle (saves a server→client replication), attach with SetParticleControlEnt + PATTACH_POINT_FOLLOW to model attach points (attach_weapon, attach_hitloc), then self:AddParticle(id, ...) so it's torn down automatically on modifier removal.

patterns.name: Custom projectile with a self-driven particle
patterns.summary: When ProjectileManager isn't enough, drive your own particle as the projectile.
patterns.technique: CreateParticle(fx, PATTACH_CUSTOMORIGIN, nil); each frame SetParticleControl(id, 0, pos) and CP1 = velocity, plus SetParticleControlForward(id, 0, dir) so the FX faces travel. Tracking variants bind CP1 to the target via SetParticleControlEnt(...PATTACH_POINT_FOLLOW,'attach_hitloc'). Store the id to DestroyParticle on hit/expire. For most cases prefer the built-in ProjectileManager:CreateLinearProjectile / CreateTrackingProjectile.

patterns.name: Game feel: screen shake, camera, hit feedback
patterns.summary: Small touches that make hits land: shake, camera nudges, and audio shaping.
patterns.technique: ScreenShake(center, amplitude, frequency, duration, radius, command, bAir) for impacts; PlayerResource:SetCameraTarget(pid, unit) (then nil to release) for cinematics/cutscenes; pair channel sounds with an explicit StopSound on release; EmitSoundOnLocationWithCaster for positional impacts; EmitGlobalSound for non-positional 'wave incoming' beats.

patterns.name: Unit AI think loop with radius targeting
patterns.summary: Per-unit AI for towers/creeps/bosses without the heavy built-in AI.
patterns.technique: Attach a thinker (AddNewModifier with OnIntervalThink, or unit:SetContextThink) running every ~0.1-0.5s; inside, FindUnitsInRadius(team, origin, nil, range, TARGET_TEAM_ENEMY, TARGET_HERO|BASIC, flags, FIND_CLOSEST, false) to pick a target, then CastAbilityOnTarget / MoveToPosition. Multi-phase bosses switch ability sets at HP thresholds. Guard authoritative logic with IsServer() and always validate handles (not :IsNull()).
patterns.source: corpus (Horde Mode, Life In Arena, Warchasers AI)

patterns.name: EHP rescale — bypass Dota's 32-bit health cap
patterns.summary: Make a boss behave/display as having billions of HP while the engine only tracks 0..200000.
patterns.technique: Clamp real MaxHealth to e.g. 200000; compute EHP_MULT = goalHP/200000; attach a modifier returning MODIFIER_PROPERTY_INCOMING_DAMAGE_PERCENTAGE = -(1 - 1/EHP_MULT)*100 so each real HP soaks EHP_MULT damage; divide base health regen by EHP_MULT; a think loop multiplies real GetHealth()/GetMaxHealth() by EHP_MULT and ships the 'effective' (comma-formatted) values to a custom Panorama HP bar. Beats the int cap instead of fighting it.

patterns.name: Rubber-banding catch-up (Mario-Kart 'blue shell')
patterns.summary: Keep races/chases close by making whoever is behind faster.
patterns.technique: Each second, rank players by remaining course distance, then assign an ASCENDING SetBaseMoveSpeed (1st = base, each next rank +N) so the last place is fastest. Capture the leader's id in the same pass to aim targeted hazards at the front-runner. Fully server-side.

patterns.name: Track-progress scalar over a waypoint course
patterns.summary: A single comparable 'how far along' number for racing/escort/lap ordering.
patterns.technique: Precompute pairwise distances between ordered checkpoints once. Each tick, per player walk checkpoints from last→first: add the full precomputed segment for each not-yet-crossed checkpoint, then add the live vector distance to the next uncrossed checkpoint and break. Sort the resulting remaining-distance scalars into positions.

patterns.name: Procedurally generated, guaranteed-solvable layout
patterns.summary: Randomize a hazard grid (minefield/maze) while guaranteeing a path exists by construction.
patterns.technique: Fill a grid as blocked; carve a guaranteed corridor with a biased random walk from one edge (each step: advance a column ~60%, else jiggle the row in-bounds), marking visited cells safe until it exits; plant hazards only on the still-blocked cells (optionally with staggered spawn timing). Solvable every round without running a solver.

patterns.name: Reuse Dota's native red error popup
patterns.summary: Show custom localized messages in Valve's familiar top-center error toast — no custom panel.
patterns.technique: $.DispatchEvent('DOTAHUDShowError', ...) / fire the dota_hud_error_message with a reason code (e.g. reason 80) and your localized string, so custom validation errors look native.

patterns.name: Read the player's REAL keybind / ping
patterns.summary: Get the user's actual configured hotkey and live ping into your UI/vscript.
patterns.technique: Keybind: create a throwaway <DOTAHotkey> panel bound to a command and read back the resolved key text to label custom controls with the player's own binding. Ping: read the text of the engine's built-in NetGraph HUD label (traverse to it) to surface real ping in vscript/UI.
patterns.api: <DOTAHotkey> panel, root-panel traversal of the NetGraph label
patterns.source: Petri Reborn / Angel Arena Black Star / training polygon

patterns.name: World→minimap overlay (custom map markers)
patterns.summary: Draw custom markers/zones on Dota's native minimap.
patterns.technique: Server projects world positions to minimap percentages (per team) and pushes them via a net table; the client places point panels sized to the native minimap element (found via HUD traversal), e.g. a battle-royale shrinking-zone circle or objective pings. Also: discover the playable bounds at runtime with TraceHull from the 4/6 sides toward center.
patterns.source: Battle of Mirkwood / Angel Arena Black Star / WTF+

patterns.name: Reliable client→server events (ACK + retry)
patterns.summary: Make Dota's lossy custom events reliable + anti-spoofed.
patterns.technique: Tag each client→server event with a sequence id + a per-client token; the server ACKs by id; the client retries unacked events with adaptive RTT backoff. The token (e.g. a per-client murmurhash the server learns passively, or one derived from GetDedicatedServerKeyV3) authenticates the sender and routes the reply. Pair with HMAC-SHA1 (canonical sorted-param string) for HTTP backend calls.

patterns.name: Custom physics: broadphase + CCD
patterns.summary: Real collision for projectiles/units beyond ProjectileManager.
patterns.technique: Maintain a custom physics world: sweep-and-prune broadphase (sort AABBs on an axis, only test overlapping spans) with continuous collision detection via a binary heap of next-collision times; re-integrate Dota's pathing velocity into the same world for hybrid movement; fly projectiles along an arc-length-reparameterized analytic curve injected as velocity; bounce off terrain using an offline-baked wall-normal 'angle grid' for O(1) reflection.
patterns.api: custom physics (SAP/CCD, binary heap), GetGroundHeight, precomputed normal grid

patterns.name: Bot AI frameworks (blackboard / potential fields)
patterns.summary: Structured bot AI beyond ad-hoc think loops.
patterns.technique: Stack-based hierarchical AI with a shared blackboard and a multi-agent supervisor (push/pop behaviors, shared world state); or potential-field gradient-descent dodging (sum repulsion vectors from threats/projectiles, step down the gradient) for reactive evasion.

patterns.name: Native Panorama drag-drop inventory + context menus
patterns.summary: A full custom inventory with item swapping and right-click menus, using Panorama's drag-drop.
patterns.technique: Use DragStart/OnDragEnter/OnDragDrop panel events (SetDraggable) to swap item slots; build right-click menus by gutting/reusing the native ContextMenuScript panel; restyle native buff/debuff icons in place. Reuse DOTAItemImage for icons + native tooltips.

patterns.name: GridNav → text export pipeline
patterns.summary: Export the map's walkability + geometry for an offline data pipeline.
patterns.technique: From vscript, sample GridNav:IsTraversable / IsBlocked over the map to build a blockage bitmap, plus a serialized wall-normal angle grid, and write them via InitLogFile/console output; bake the result into a packed runtime grid (RLE / packed ints) the game loads instead of recomputing.
patterns.source: Battle of Characters / Battle of Mirkwood

patterns.name: Line-level Lua memory profiler
patterns.summary: Find allocation hotspots in vscript during development.
patterns.technique: Install a debug.sethook(fn, 'l') line hook that samples collectgarbage('count') deltas per source line, accumulating allocation per (file:line); dump the worst offenders. A dev-only tool to hunt GC churn that causes hitches.

patterns.name: Centralized modifier event-bus / fan-out
patterns.summary: One global thinker re-dispatches engine MODIFIER_EVENT_* to the modifiers that care — instead of every modifier declaring its own events.
patterns.technique: Attach a single thinker modifier to the game-mode entity that DeclareFunctions() ALL the MODIFIER_EVENT_* (OnTakeDamage/OnAttack/OnDeath/...). On modifier-added, reflect over the new modifier against an event→method map and subscribe it to only the events it implements; re-dispatch each engine event to the registered listener list (auto-pruning IsNull()). Lets you add synthetic events the engine doesn't expose (e.g. OnHealDone) and gives modifiers a zero-boilerplate 'self events' API (OnParentTakeDamage/OnParentDealDamage). Cheaper than N modifiers each evaluated globally.
patterns.source: Ability Arena modifier_event_fan_out.lua; Kill Boss And Win gameplay_event_tracker

patterns.name: MMO-style threat/aggro table for boss targeting
patterns.summary: WoW-style tank aggro layered on Dota AI — abilities/items add or drop threat; the boss targets highest-threat with hysteresis + stickiness.
patterns.technique: Each hero carries a numeric threat; abilities/items declare an AbilityThreat in KV (e.g. an item with -25 = a taunt-drop). The boss targets the highest-threat hero but only switches when a rival exceeds the current by a margin (5 * personality) — hysteresis — and keeps the target until ~1.5s of lost vision — stickiness. Rank heroes (primary/secondary) and push each player their aggro rank for a UI indicator.

patterns.name: Custom ability/item metadata via a KV table (extend the schema, not code)
patterns.summary: Attach arbitrary gameplay metadata to abilities/items in their KV that the engine ignores, read through a single lookup.
patterns.technique: Pre-parse all ability/item KV into GameRules.AbilityKV[name]; add accessor methods (CDOTABaseAbility:GetThreat() etc.) that read CUSTOM keys (AbilityThreat, PiercesDisableReduction, InnateAbility, …) the engine has no concept of. Designers tune systems purely in data; works for both datadriven + lua abilities.

patterns.name: Declarative target-selection DSL for ability auto-cast
patterns.summary: A composable filter-chain rules engine that picks ability targets across hundreds of abilities (auto-battler).
patterns.technique: Each ability registers ordered ruleSets; a rule is a predicate (target, ctx, remaining)->bool that narrows candidates, composed with and/or/not. Primitives: isClosest/Farthest, isLowest/HighestHealth, hasEnemyInRange, modifier-stack checks, plus arena-relative geometry via signed distance to a line. Auto-derive sane targeting from the ability's KV (team + behavior) when no rules are given; cluster predicted unit positions for ground-target cast points (lead targeting).
patterns.api: data-driven rule predicates, FindUnitsInRadius, signed-distance geometry

patterns.name: Queryable state-sync over custom events (net-table alternative)
patterns.summary: Reactive client/server state without CustomNetTables — subscribe to named 'parts', server pushes only changed ones, with spectator indirection.
patterns.technique: Server defineQueryableState(name, fn, hashKey); a think loop hashes each part and Send_ServerToPlayer('stateUpdate', changed) only on change. Client subscribeToQuery([parts], handler) requests missing parts, caches them, and fires the handler only when ALL parts are present (a reactive multi-key join). Serialize the VIEWED player's state to the actual client so spectating reuses the UI unchanged; bots are headless clients using the same API.

patterns.name: Diff-based per-player nested-state replication
patterns.summary: Ship only deltas of a nested state tree per player, with delete/clear sentinels — a scalable net-table alternative.
patterns.technique: Keep a full snapshot + an incremental diff tree per player keyed by dotted paths; debounce at ~1/30s and send only deltas. Encode deletions as a sentinel object ({_remove=N}) and subtree replacement as a __bClear flag the client honors. Cut event count by GROUPING all players that share an identical value into one space-delimited 'players' string per event. Auto-prime late joiners via a request/response handshake.

patterns.name: Income economy: progressive tax + diminishing returns
patterns.summary: The auto-spawn AoS economy (Castle Fight): periodic income with a progressive tax curve so leaders don't snowball.
patterns.technique: Grant periodic income per player; apply a progressive 'tax' (higher income taxed more) and diminishing returns on repeated same-building spend so the gap between leaders and trailers stays bounded — a soft rubber-band done through the economy instead of stats.

patterns.name: Population-adaptive AI think-rate throttling (load shedding)
patterns.summary: Scale per-unit AI think interval to the live unit count so big battles don't tank the server.
patterns.technique: Track the number of active AI units; widen each unit's think interval as population grows (and/or process a round-robin slice of units per tick) so total AI work per frame stays bounded. Trade reaction latency for frame time only when crowded.
patterns.api: SetContextThink with a dynamic return interval, round-robin slicing

patterns.name: Warcraft-3 attack-type × armor-type damage matrix
patterns.summary: Reproduce WC3's attack-type vs armor-type damage multipliers (piercing/siege/magic vs light/heavy/fortified) on top of Dota.
patterns.technique: Tag units with an attack-type and an armor-type; in a damage filter (SetDamageFilter) look up a 2D multiplier matrix by (attackType, armorType) and scale the damage before it applies. Lets you port classic AoS/TD damage triangles.

patterns.name: Roguelite affix ('elite mob') system
patterns.summary: Diablo/PoE-style random affixes on wave units, color-coded so players read them at a glance.
patterns.technique: A pool of ~18 affix modifiers (reflect, lifesteal-on-hit, explode-on-death, DoT-on-hit, regen aura, giant…); each affix's OnCreated SetRenderColor()s the unit a distinct tint. Regular packs roll one affix; bosses run a roll-and-dedup loop (HasModifier check, break at N) for exactly N DISTINCT affixes. A reusable elite-mob framework.

patterns.name: Asymptotic diminishing-returns stacking
patterns.summary: Combine multiple sources of a 0..1 stat (reduction/evasion) so they stack like Dota's real reductions and never exceed 100%.
patterns.technique: Apply stacked modifier sources in a fixed operation order (SET → ADD → MULT → PCT → ASYMPTOTE); the asymptote op is value + next − value*next (multiplicative diminishing returns). Recompute per ability level and ship the result to clients for accurate tooltips.

patterns.name: Party-aware team balancer + MMR comeback
patterns.summary: Auto-balance teams without splitting premades, then rubber-band the weaker team by real MMR.
patterns.technique: Enumerate every split of N players into two halves (lexicographic combination iterator); score each by a two-key objective: FIRST minimize premade parties split across teams (GetPartyID), THEN minimize skill-sum delta; apply with SetCustomTeamAssignment during setup. Separately, compute a team skill ratio from a backend (game_count/win_rate) and give the weaker team time-scaling free upgrades + a gold multiplier.

patterns.name: Single-source data pipeline (spreadsheet → KV + JSON)
patterns.summary: Author balance data once and generate both the server KV (Lua) and the client lookup (JSON).
patterns.technique: Keep the source of truth in a spreadsheet (Excel/CSV); a build step emits the npc/ability KV (or a Lua table) for the server AND a JSON/JS lookup for Panorama, so tooltips/costs/icons never drift from gameplay values. Regenerate as part of the build.

patterns.name: Co-op proximity revive (beacon)
patterns.summary: Dead players drop a beacon any teammate can channel/stand near to revive them — a co-op staple.
patterns.technique: On death, spawn a beacon entity (a marker/'X') at the corpse and a per-frame check: if a living teammate is within radius for the required time, revive the dead player at the beacon and remove it. Show the beacon + a progress hint on the HUD/minimap.

patterns.name: Runtime ability-graft onto engine-owned entities (self-healing)
patterns.summary: Attach custom behavior to entities you can't define in KV (fountains, shops) and re-apply it across engine resets.
patterns.technique: You can't edit ent_dota_fountain etc. in script — instead a GlobalThink (~1s) re-scans Entities:FindAllByClassname and, for any missing your ability, AddAbility()+SetLevel() to (re)graft it. The recurring check makes it self-healing across fountain/shop rebuilds at game-state transitions (a one-time setup silently fails). The grafted ability can be an aura/passive (e.g. an anti-fountain-camp silence aura).

patterns.name: Reuse a built-in hero modifier as a stasis/hide primitive
patterns.summary: Repurpose an existing engine ability's modifier as a general 'remove unit from the world' building block.
patterns.technique: AddNewModifier(..., 'modifier_phoenix_supernova_hiding') hides + untargets a unit (e.g. stash an AFK/disconnected hero so the opponent can't farm it). Re-apply it with {duration = 0.00} to instantly clear a no-duration modifier and bring the unit back. Generalize: many built-in modifiers are reusable primitives (stun, invuln, phase) — link/apply them instead of writing your own.
patterns.api: AddNewModifier (built-in modifier names), duration=0 to clear

patterns.name: Arena/duel round reset (cooldowns) on kill credit
patterns.summary: Refresh everyone's full arsenal each round for round-based arenas.
patterns.technique: Listen to dota_team_kill_credit (fires once per SCORED kill, unlike entity_killed); on it, walk all heroes and EndCooldown() each non-passive ability (slots 0-15) AND each item (slots 0-5) so duelists re-engage with everything ready. Also the canonical hook for round bookkeeping/score.

patterns.name: Off-mesh terrain traversal (controlled cliff-climbing)
patterns.summary: Let a ground unit cross blocked terrain in a punishing, exploit-resistant way.
patterns.technique: A per-tick modifier toggles MODIFIER_STATE_FLYING_FOR_PATHING_PURPOSES_ONLY so the unit can step onto GridNav:IsBlocked cells; record the last good position and SetAbsOrigin back if a tick ends on blocked ground (rubber-band). While on blocked ground, apply overlapping decaying movespeed penalties (e.g. -200/1.1s, -50/3s, -25/5s) so it crawls across and pays a fading tax — instead of a hard wall or instant teleport.

patterns.name: Destructible trees as asymmetric level geometry
patterns.summary: Use the map's living trees as dynamic, self-healing terrain — central to tag/maze/stealth modes.
patterns.technique: Query trees per-frame with GridNav:GetAllTreesAroundPoint + tree:IsStanding. Give teams opposite relationships: the hider gets speed + NOT_ON_MINIMAP while in trees (forest = cover); the hunter must tree:CutDownRegrowAfter(t, team) to chop a path and is self-slowed while doing so. SetTreeRegrowTime makes the maze regrow/heal, so foliage IS the geometry, not decoration.

patterns.name: Client-side grid placement UI (tower/building)
patterns.summary: Warcraft-3-style build placement with per-cell validity + a model ghost, computed entirely on the client.
patterns.technique: Each tick: snap the cursor to the grid, and for the 4 corner cells WorldToScreenX/Y → GameUI.FindScreenEntities(pixel) to see what occupies each, tinting that corner's ghost particle red/green (partial-cell feedback, no server round-trip). Preview an arbitrary unit MODEL with a server-cached EF_NODRAW dummy (modifier_out_of_world) whose entIndex the client binds a ghost_model.vpcf to via SetParticleControlEnt(PATTACH_ABSORIGIN_FOLLOW); add a radius_indicator.vpcf sized to attack range.
patterns.api: Game.WorldToScreenX/Y, GameUI.FindScreenEntities, Particles (ghost model + range), EF_NODRAW dummy

patterns.name: Native engine systems as free RTS UI
patterns.summary: Overload Dota's stat/mana systems to render RTS resources + build bars with zero Panorama.
patterns.technique: Store extra economies in the hero's three attributes (Intellect=Energy, Strength=Tech, Agility=Income) so the stock attribute HUD shows them for free (keep an authoritative _G table in sync, mirror via SetBase*). Drive a unit's mana 0..100 as a circular progress/cooldown bar: spawn cadence = SetMana(tick/rate*100); a 'channel' fills exactly over a duration via SetMana(0)+SetBaseManaRegen(GetMaxMana()/time).

patterns.name: Production queue in a building's item-inventory slots
patterns.summary: Implement an RTS train/research queue using the unit's native 6-slot item bar (free drag/cancel UI).
patterns.technique: Each queued order is an item named item_<train_ability>: enqueue = CreateItem+AddItem (cap 6); dequeue removes + refunds; advance by scanning slots 0-5, stripping the 'item_' prefix to find the paired ability and channeling it (EndChannel → spawn). ReorderItems/SwapItems compacts the slots so the inventory bar IS the visible ordered queue.

patterns.name: Client-side selection rewriting (RTS control groups)
patterns.summary: Make box-select prioritize army over buildings (and other custom selection rules) in Panorama.
patterns.technique: Subscribe to dota_player_update_selected_unit; when a drag-select mixes buildings + units, rebuild the selection with GameUI.SelectUnit to keep only non-buildings. Guard recursion: each programmatic SelectUnit re-fires the event, so set a module-level skip flag before every call and re-emit the corrected selection on a $.Schedule debounce.

patterns.name: In-place tier upgrade by unit replacement
patterns.summary: Tower/building tiers via destroy-and-recreate, with the next unit's name derived by string math.
patterns.technique: Upgrade = create the next-tier unit (name from a '_level_N' convention: append '_level_2', else increment the trailing digit), carry over platform/owner refs, re-level abilities by index, park the old unit (z=-512) and destroy it next tick. Gate tiers on the builder hero's level/XP (AddExperience + ability SetLevel); the same Lua class is aliased across all per-unit upgrade ability defs. Build progress itself = spawn at 1 HP and lerp SetHealth→max over BuildTime (reuses the health bar as a build timer).
patterns.api: CreateUnitByName, string name math, SetHealth lerp, AddExperience

patterns.name: Bitboard flood-fill maze validator
patterns.summary: Answer 'does this tower wall off the lane?' orders of magnitude cheaper than A*, using packed-grid bitwise BFS.
patterns.technique: Encode the buildable grid as a bitboard (each row = integer 'segments', SEGMENT_SIZE≈29 usable bits + reserved overflow bits, one bit per column). Flood from every exit: per row, expand a column each side with pathSeg = bor(pathSeg, lshift(pathSeg,1), rshift(pathSeg,1)) then clip to pathable with band(pathSeg, testSeg); propagate up/down rows to a fixpoint; reserved boundary bits drive cross-segment overflow. Then verify every gateway/unit still touches the mask. A cheap QuickCheck first walks the footprint perimeter and allows placement only if blocked cells form a single contiguous arc.
patterns.api: LuaJIT bit ops (bor/band/lshift/rshift/bdiff), packed row segments

patterns.name: Commit-then-validate anti-maze (engine pathfinding)
patterns.summary: Simplest maze-prevention: let the engine place the blocker, then check real pathing and roll back if a lane is cut.
patterns.technique: After BuildingHelper places the point_simple_obstruction for a finished building, call GridNav:CanFindPath(spawner, waypoint) for every lane; if ANY fails (player walled a lane), RemoveBuilding + refund the gold + SendErrorMessage. 'Commit, validate actual engine pathfinding, undo on failure' — no parallel grid model to maintain (vs the proactive bitboard/A* validators).

patterns.name: Reactive build-tree dependency graph (dirty propagation)
patterns.summary: Recompute only the tech/build nodes whose inputs changed, instead of re-evaluating the whole tree each tick.
patterns.technique: Author prerequisites as a recursive DSL in KV (and/or/nand/nor blocks, per-level gates, '@' self-ref, named refs, leaf aspect/variable comparisons). Statically crawl each node's require tree to extract the set of leaf 'triggers' it depends on, and register a refcounted reverse index requireTriggers[trigger] = {dependents}. On a state-change event, look up only the nodes depending on that trigger and recompute just those — a spreadsheet-recalc / dirty-propagation model for tech trees.
patterns.api: recursive requirement DSL, reverse trigger index, event-driven recompute

patterns.name: Spatial decay penalty field (sell-refund diffusion)
patterns.summary: Discourage resell/abuse near a hot spot with a penalty that floods outward and decays with distance.
patterns.technique: On sell, bump 'stacks' on the footprint tiles (batch same-tick sells via one timer). Push over-threshold tiles into a PriorityQueue keyed by stacks; pop highest-first and propagate a decremented value into the 4-adjacent free tiles, re-pushing them so the penalty floods outward. Tiles use a lazy 'bump' snapshot (recompute from one timestamp instead of ticking). A reusable spatial-decay field built on batched priority diffusion.

patterns.name: WC3 corpse-as-entity system
patterns.summary: Make corpses queryable map entities so all raise/consume abilities share one corpse pool (Warcraft-3 style).
patterns.technique: On any non-hero/non-building death, spawn a persistent INVISIBLE npc_dota_creature at the death spot set to the corpse model, and stamp Lua fields on the handle: unit_name = the dead unit's name, corpse_expiration = time+N (self-removes after). Raise Dead / Animate Dead / Resurrection / Spirit of Vengeance all Entities:FindAllByNameWithin('npc_dota_creature', origin, radius) and reconstruct by the stamped unit_name.
